title: Dalea mountjoyae
intro: Dalea mountjoyae, also known as Mountjoy prairie clover, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Dalea mountjoyae include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support women's health, particularly in relation to menopause symptoms and menstrual irregularities.
taxonomy: Dalea mountjoyae is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Dalea and is part of the tribe Genisteae. This plant is native to Central America, specifically in the tropical regions of Mexico and Guatemala. Further classification within the Fabaceae family includes its placement in the subfamily Faboideae. As part of the Genisteae tribe, Dalea mountjoyae is closely related to other legume species within this tribe.
